title: Macroeconomic and Foreign Exchange Policies of Major Trading Partners of the United States
summary: This page is a navigation index listing historical Treasury semiannual Reports to Congress on international economic and exchange rate policies, spanning from 1989 to 2025. No substantive report content is present in this source; only report titles and dates are listed. The most recent reports referenced are June 2025 and November 2024.
key_points: This is a listing/navigation page, not an actual report or policy document.
Historical reports are listed from 1989 through 2025.
Reports are authorized under 2015 and 1988 statutes.
The most recent links are June 2025 Report and November 2024 Report.
No macro data, exchange rate determinations, or policy conclusions are present in this source.
An annex on Foreign Exchange Reserve Accumulation is referenced.
Appendices on reserve currency status, IMF surveillance, sovereign wealth funds, and cross-border capital flows appear in historical reports.
why_it_matters: This page serves only as a directory to Treasury's semiannual exchange rate policy reports. Actual macro catalyst information—such as Treasury findings on currency manipulation, exchange rate assessments, or trade partner policies—would be contained in the individual reports themselves, not this index page. This source cannot be used for market research as it contains no substantive policy content.
notes: Non-archiveable listing page. This URL hosts an index of historical Treasury reports on exchange rate policy but contains no actual report text, data, or conclusions. Researchers seeking macro catalyst content should access specific report links (e.g., June 2025 Report) rather than this navigation page. The listing page itself provides no actionable information for market structure or macro analysis.

reasoning: Navigation/listing page with no substantive content
Cannot classify event_type without actual report content
Does not meet criteria for research archive inclusion
